question_text:
# TrafficVision: Inception-based Traffic Sign Classifier

Build an Inception-inspired CNN to classify 32x32 RGB traffic sign images into 10 categories.

## What You Need to Do

1. Load the dataset from `traffic_signs.npz` and create PyTorch `Dataset` and `DataLoader` objects
2. Build a custom `InceptionModule` (`nn.Module` subclass) with four parallel branches:
   - Branch 1: 1x1 convolution
   - Branch 2: 1x1 conv (bottleneck) followed by 3x3 conv
   - Branch 3: 1x1 conv (bottleneck) followed by 5x5 conv
   - Branch 4: 3x3 MaxPool followed by 1x1 conv
   - Concatenate all branch outputs along the channel dimension
3. Build a classifier using at least one InceptionModule, ending with FC layers for 10-class output
4. Train using CrossEntropyLoss and achieve >= 70% accuracy on unseen test data

## Expected Output

| Variable | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `model` | `nn.Module` | Trained classifier with at least one Inception module |

test_cases.display_text: The solution should run without any errors
A trained PyTorch model (nn.Module) should exist in your code
The model should use at least 3 different convolution kernel sizes (e.g., 1x1, 3x3, 5x5) to capture multi-scale features
The model should have at least 3 Conv2d layers with kernel_size=1 for dimensionality reduction (bottleneck convolutions)
The model should produce an output with 10 units when given a (1, 3, 32, 32) input tensor
The model should achieve at least 70% classification accuracy on the hidden test set
